session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: (laughing)

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: All the speculations about Jesus. He was black, he was Asian, he was a blue-eyed Aryan with a golden beard and hair straight out of Vidal Sassoon's, he was a benevolent alien, he never existed at all.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: ...Now he's a caveman.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: (laughs)

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: The Christ figure goes all the way back to Krishna- Hercules, of course.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: Hercules?

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: Born of a virgin: Alcmene. A god for a father: Zeus. The only begotten. The savior- The Greek?

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: "Soter". The Good Shepherd, the Prince of Peace, bringing gentle persuasion and divine wisdom. He died, joined his father on Olympus a thousand years before Gethsemane.

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: How can you compare Pagan mythology to the true word?

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: Pretty damn closely, I'd say.

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: The early Christian leaders, they threw away Hebrew manuscripts and borrowed from Pagan sources all over the place.

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: Do you realize how...inconsiderately you're treating my feelings?

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: About as inconsiderately as we're treating John's.

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: Well, he does- he doesn't believe what he's saying!

session_6.speaker: Sandy
session_6.text: Do you believe literally everything in the bible, Edith?

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: Yes!

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: ...N- Uh- Before you say it, I know it's undergone a lot of changes, but God has spoken through man to make his word clearer.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: He couldn't get it right the first time?

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: We-we're imperfect! He had t-to work to make us understand.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: He couldn't get us right the first time, Edith?

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: Taken alone, the philosophical teachings of Jesus are Buddhism with a Hebrew accent- Kindness, tolerance, brotherhood, love... A ruthless realism acknowledging that life is as it is here on earth, here and now.

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: The Kingdom of God, meaning...goodness, is...is right here, where it should be. "I am what I am becoming." That's what the Buddha brought in.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: And that's what I taught. But a talking snake make a lady eat an apple, so we're screwed.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: Heaven and Hell were peddled so priests could rule through seduction and terror...save our souls that we never lost in the first place.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: I threw a clean pass...they ran it out of the ballpark.

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: This is blasphemy. It's horrible! Who else were you? Solomon? Elvis? Jack the Ripper?

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: It's been said that Buddha and Jesus would laugh or cry if they'd known what was done in their name.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: And if there is a creator, he'd probably feel the same way.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: I see ceremony, ritual, processions, genuflecting, moaning, intoning, venerating cookies and wine.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: And I think...it's not what I had in mind.

session_6.speaker: Edith
session_6.text: But that's Vatican flapdoodle. It doesn't have a thing to do with God.

session_6.speaker: Dan
session_6.text: As you said, John. Everywhere, religions...from exalting life to purging joy as a sin. Rome does it as grand opera. A simple path to goodness needs a supernatural roadmap.

session_6.speaker: Harry
session_6.text: Supernatural...

session_6.speaker: Art
session_6.text: A stupid word, I mean...anything that happens, happens within nature, whether we believe in it or not.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: (looks up)

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: ...Like a 14,000-year-old caveman.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: The sound of a car approaching...

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: John heads outside to receive Will.

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: I--I--I drove for a while, and then I sat for a while. I'm so ashamed.

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: (Shivering)

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: And I'm freezing!

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: Well, come inside.

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: I still don't believe you, of course. You need help.

session_6.speaker: John
session_6.text: Everybody needs help.

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: Yes, well, some more than others.

session_6.speaker: Will
session_6.text: Fade out.

session_7.text: Scene 1a: Eventide
Fade in, Will is warming himself in front of the fire, it is darker.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: From the Buddha to the cross, I have always imagined both as entirely mythic. Ye- but I would like to hear more.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: May I lie on the couch for a moment? I'm not as young as I used to be.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: (plonks himself on the couch)

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Ohh! Hah, so! You were Jesus. Well, perhaps somebody had to be, for better or for worse. The jury is still out.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: When did you begin to believe you were Jesus?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: When did you begin to believe you were a psychiatrist?

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Since I graduated Harvard medical school and finished my residency, I've had that feeling.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Oh, I sometimes dream about it.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Have you acted upon this belief?

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: I had a private practice for a while, and then I taught. Nothing unusual- Oh, until one day, I met a caveman who thought he was Jesus.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Do you find that unusual?

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Very. I would stake my reputation he is as sane as I am, so why does he persist in such a story?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: There must be a reason, though.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Unless I imagined it all? Is that possible?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I think you're as sane as he is.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Oh, god, I- (laughs)

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: No.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Did you ever find it prudent to worship yourself rather than be thought a heretic? That would be something!

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: At the time Christianity was considered heresy, I had to pretend other faiths.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: And what does Jesus have to say to those present who find it difficult to believe in Him?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Believe in what he tried to teach, without rigmarole. Piety is not what the lessons bring to people. It's the mistake they bring to the lessons.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: (slowly getting up and putting on his jacket)

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Well, it's getting to be night. I still have stuff to carry and a long drive.

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: I'll help.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: John, do you have a destination in mind?

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Never mind. I won't ask.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Thank you.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: (exits with Sandy, carrying boxes)
Scene 1b: Debate

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Anyone mentally ill can imagine a fantastic background- Even an entire life- and sincerely believe it. The man who thinks he is Napoleon does believe it. His true identity has taken a backseat to his delusion and the need for it. If that's the case with John, there is a grave disorder.

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: Organized brilliantly. He's got an answer for everything.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: It might involve rejection of his father, of his entire early past, replaced by this fantasy.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: He says he can't remember his father.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Precisely. Why?

session_7.speaker: Linda
session_7.text: You said he was sane.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Did I?

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Do you think that perhaps our caveman has a monkey on his back?

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: Drugs?

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: No, no, no, no. I've done a lot of consulting work with the narcotics division. I've seen people...tripping, strung out- Whatever's up with John, it isn't that. I've looked for signs- None.

session_7.speaker: Linda
session_7.text: Could cavemen really talk?

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: We think that language came into existence sixty thousand years ago. The structure of Stone Age culture is evidence of the ability to communicate... verbally.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Harry wolf whistles, clicks his tongue.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Oh, shut up.
Scene 1c: Sandy
John is looking at the stars.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Maybe it'd be easier if I were.

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: Crazy? No.

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: Coyote howls. John then looks at Sandy.
Scene 2: The Last Spell

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: That is fascinating, though, isn't it, a brave attempt to teach Buddhism in the West. It's no wonder he failed. We're not ready for it.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: You're talking as if you believed him.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Well, it is possible, isn't it? I mean, anything is possible.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Look, we have two simple choices. We can get all bent out of shape intellectualizing or bench- pressing logic, or we can simply relax and enjoy it. I can listen critically, but I don't have to make up my mind about anything. But you think you do?

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: Well, unfortunately, there's no authorities on prehistory, so we couldn't stop him there.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: There are experts on the bible.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Chheh, dream on.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Yeah, thus the lost years of Jesus. He didn't exist until John put on a hat.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: I don't believe in angels and the nativity and the star in the East, but there are stories about the childhood of Jesus.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: History hates a vacuum. Improvisation, some of it very sincere, fills the gaps. It would have been easy to falsify a past back then- A few words, credulity- Time would do the rest.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: Now you're talking as if you believed him.

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: Well, look at the popular myths surrounding the Kennedy assassination in a few short years- You had, uh, conspiracy, mafia, CIA- That's a mystique that'll never go away.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: It's always been a small step from a fallen leader to a god.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: I don't think anybody will deify Kennedy. We're more sophisticated than that.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: We are?

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: We are.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: John and Sandy enter.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Well, you're finally fulfilling one prophecy about the millennium, John.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: What's that?

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Here you are again.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: John makes himself comfortable between Sandy and the fireplace.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: You like the fire, John.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Everywhere I've lived, I've had a fireplace.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Childhood fixation, I guess. Helps me to feel secure.

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: (smiling)

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: There are predators out there.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: One thing I didn't pack...I thought I might need it.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: John puts on a tape/record of Beethoven's 7th Symphony.

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: Wouldn't Sacre du Printemps be more appropriate?

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: What?

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: You've got...four men of science completely baffled, my friend. We- We don't know what to make of you.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Did you know Voltaire was the first to suggest that the universe was created by a gigantic explosion? I think Paul would agree. And then Goethe was the first to suggest that spiral nebulae were swirling masses of stars. We now call them galaxies. It's kind of funny how often new concepts of science find their first tentative forms of expression in the arts.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: So did Beethoven do physics on the side?

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: He spent most of his time lying on the floor in front of his legless piano surrounded by orange peels and apple cores.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Now we're on the floor listening to Beethoven.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Full circle.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Did you have, um... any religious beliefs? Or didn't you give it much thought?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: You can't get there with thought.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: You have faith?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: In a lot of things.

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: Do you have faith in the future of the race?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I've seen species come and go. Depends on their balance with the environment.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: We've made a mess of it.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: There's still time, if we use it well.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: Christianity has been a worldwide belief for 2,000 years.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: How long did the Egyptians worship Isis? Or the Sumerians Ishtar? In India, sacred cows wandered freely as reincarnated souls. In a thousand years, they'll be barbecued and their souls will be in squirrels.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: (firmly)

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: You weren't Jesus!

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: John smiles.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: Oh, Edith.

session_7.speaker: Will
session_7.text: (rises to his feet)

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: (clearing her throat)

session_7.speaker: Sandy
session_7.text: If it rains, y-...

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: (interrupting)

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: -It won't.

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: How do you know that?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I don't smell it.

session_7.speaker: Linda
session_7.text: Were you... I guess a... medicine man?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I was a shaman a few times. I… revealed some truths to eat a little better.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: You think that's all religion is about...

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: selling hope and survival?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: The Old Testament sells fear and guilt. The New Testament is a good code of ethics, put into my mouth by poets and philosophers that are much smarter than I am. The message is never practiced. Fairy tales build churches.

session_7.speaker: Art
session_7.text: What about the name "Jesus"? Did you pull that out of a hat?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I called myself John. I almost always do.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: As tales of the resurrection spread, the name was confused with the Hebrew "Yochanan", meaning "God is gracious". My stay on earth was seen as divine proof of immortality. That led to "God is salvation", or Hebrew "Yahshua", which in translation became my proper name, changing to late Greek, "Iesous", Then to late Latin, "Iesus", and finally medieval Latin, "Jesus".

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: And it was a wonder to watch it all happen.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: Then you didn't claim to be the Son of God?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Began as a schoolhouse and ended as a temple. I said I had a master that was greater than myself. I never said he was my father. I wanted to teach what I learned. I never claimed to be king of the Jews, I never walked on water, I never raised the dead. I never spoke of divine except in the sense of human goodness on earth.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: (pause)

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: No wise men came from the East to worship at a manger. ...I did do a little healing with some eastern medicine I'd learned... That's it.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: The three wise men began as a myth about the birth of the Buddha.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: John, I should be home, uh, kissing my wife.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: We're all here, trapped by your story...hoping for a...revolution? I don't know.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Are there any, uh...more revelations for us?

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: John shakes his head, smiling.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: It's just like old times.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: (after a pause, glaring at John, and then firmly, again)

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: You. Weren't. Jesus.

session_7.speaker: Harry
session_7.text: Quote the Sermon on the Mount.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Which one? Darby? King James? New American Standard?

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: Do you know them all?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: No one knows the one, not even me.

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: I...I did some teaching on a hill one day. Not that many people stayed.

session_7.speaker: Dan
session_7.text: But you...

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Biblical Jesus said, "Who do you think I am?" He gave them a choice. I'm giving you one.

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: (timidly, her voice quivering, eyes tearing up)

session_7.speaker: Edith
session_7.text: W-were you?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: If I said no, could you ever be sure?

session_7.speaker: John
session_7.text: Edith's lips quiver, and she suddenly lets out a sob. Art switches the light on to break the spell.

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: It's gone much too far. These people are very upset. I don't believe you're mad, but what you're saying is not true. That leaves only one explanation. The time has come when you must admit this is a hoax... a lie. Isn't that true, John?

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: If you don't drop this now- If you can- I'll be convinced that you need a great deal of attention. I can have you committed for observation. You know that. I ask you now- I demand it- That you tell these people the truth.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: End of the line. Everybody off.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: (hands raised, rising to his feet and pacing)

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: All of it? But what- What in the name of heaven...

session_8.speaker: Art
session_8.text: John, you had us wondering whether you were sane or not, and it's just a story! Where'd you come off with such a half-baked, asinine idea?

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: At least you're relieved I'm not a nut.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: You gave me the idea. All of you.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Edith saw my fake Van Gogh.

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: You could have just told me.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: You commented that I never age. You gave me the book on early man. Dan, you spotted the burin and you said, "If stones could speak."

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: I got the notion, I ran it past you to check your reactions, and I took it too far.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: You asked if I was a figure from religious history, if there were others like me, if I'd created future identities. We were chasing our tails around the maypole, enjoying the mystery, the analytical stretch. You were playing my game! I was playing yours.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: Oh, man, you know, you had us going, right? You were good, man. You know those Chinese boxes, one, inside the other, inside the other, inside the other- I feel like I'm in the- I'm in the- I'm in the last box. You son of a...

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: BITCH! How could you do this to us?

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: I know, I was tempted to cop out many times, but I couldn't resist seeing whether or not you could refute what I was saying. I had the perfect audience- anthropologist, archaeologist, Christian literalist... A psychologist.

session_8.speaker: Art
session_8.text: Okay, I've had enough of this. I'm outta here.

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: So, John. Are you gonna write the story?

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: If I do, I'll send you copies.

session_8.speaker: Art
session_8.text: Don't bother with mine, okay? You are absolutely certifiable. I don't know you!

session_8.speaker: Linda
session_8.text: It was nice seeing you again, Dr. Oldman.

session_8.speaker: Linda
session_8.text: ...Your name's a pun, isn't it? Old man? Did that help you with your story?

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: ...Well, at least I don't have to throw away half of what I know about biology.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: It's a beautiful idea, so rich, so full of possibilities.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Perhaps you should write a paper on it, Doctor.

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: Maybe I will. I'll interview you in the rubber room for further details. You may still need help, my friend.

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: John grabs a bag and heads out. Sandy scrambles to her feet and rushes after him.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: I thought it sounded pretty good.

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: They believe you because they have to. But the one thing that I know about you is that you would never use people or abuse their goodwill and intelligence like they think you've just done to them.

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: So you're a pretty fast liar, Mr. Ugg, but I wanna know- What's your real name?

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Believe it or not, the sound was always John.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: What happened was enough. Just- Just needed to stop. I- I shouldn't have expected it to work.

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: Fourteen thousand years old. I bet that's a lot of women.

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: Well, I'm taking Edith home. Sandy?

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: ...Are you sorry for some of those things you said?

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: Well. Like a good Christian, I...

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: (hugs John, he lifts her up giving her a tight hug)

session_8.speaker: Edith
session_8.text: Oh! Well, you did a terrible thing, but we're all so thankful you're all right. Even Art- He just hates things he can't understand.

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: You're a sadist, John, but I admit I got a kick out of chasing my tail around your maypole...

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: even if that is all I caught. Good luck to you.

session_8.speaker: Harry
session_8.text: Edith puts her hand on John's face, and gives him a kiss on the cheek. They depart

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: Dan comes out, hugs Sandy from the side.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: I don't know, man. Something about this...

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: Something about you, John. The more I think about it, the more I'm no longer in that Chinese box.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: I sense...space. A kinda latitude of what we happily call reality, in which, as everybody keeps saying... anything's possible.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: No. N-no. N-no. No- No more words. I'm gonna go home, and I'm gonna watch Doctor Who for a dose of sanity. Good luck to you, man, wherever this may lead you. ...You drop me a line, sometime.

session_8.speaker: Dan
session_8.text: Let me know how you're making out.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Dan gives Sandy a friendly kiss and walks off.

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: So, John Oldman. What other pun names have you used?

session_8.speaker: Sandy
session_8.text: Will is slowly exiting, inadvertently sneaking up on them.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Lots. John Paley for John Paleolithic. John Savage- Got really crazy about 60 years ago, when I was teaching at Harvard, I was John Thomas Partee.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: John T. Partee- Boston tea party- Will who is right behind them now takes a step back with a look of shock on his face

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: Wait, wait, wait. B-Boston? 60 years ago? J-John Partee?

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: John puts a hand on his mouth.

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: You did not teach chemistry! I do not believe you!

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: My- My- My dog's name. We had him before I was born.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Sorry, I had to move on. You know that. I left enough. I left enough.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: (putting his arm around him and escorting him inside)

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Chilly Willy, always cold. Never could stand the cold.

session_8.speaker: Will
session_8.text: Wait, you- you had a beard!

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Yeah, you used to tug on it to see if it was real.

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Will! God. Call an ambulance, now!

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: (Will gasps, collapses with John holding him)

session_8.speaker: John
session_8.text: Come on, Willy. Will. Come on, buddy.
